syntax:
## Docker Cheat Sheet

### Key Concepts
- **Image**: Read-only blueprint (like a class)
- **Container**: Running instance of an image (like an object)
- **Dockerfile**: Script to build an image
- **Docker Hub**: Registry of public images
- **Volume**: Persistent storage that survives container restarts
- **Network**: How containers communicate with each other

### Essential Commands
```bash
# Build image from Dockerfile in current directory
docker build -t myapp:v1 .

# Run a container
docker run -d -p 3000:3000 --name myapp myapp:v1
  # -d: detached (background)
  # -p host:container port mapping
  # --name: give it a name

# View running containers
docker ps

# View logs
docker logs myapp
docker logs -f myapp  # follow (live)

# Stop / remove
docker stop myapp
docker rm myapp
docker rmi myapp:v1  # remove image

# Execute command inside running container
docker exec -it myapp bash
```

### Dockerfile Keywords
```dockerfile
FROM node:18-alpine      # base image
WORKDIR /app             # working directory
COPY package*.json ./    # copy files
RUN npm ci               # run command during build
EXPOSE 3000              # document port (doesn't publish)
CMD ["node", "server.js"] # default run command
```

### Docker Compose
```yaml
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports: ["3000:3000"]
    depends_on: [db]
  db:
    image: postgres:15
    environment:
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: secret
```
```bash
docker compose up -d   # start all services
docker compose down    # stop and remove
```

commonErrors.description: Calling variables outside their declared scope.

interviewNotes: Be ready to explain scoping rules and time/space characteristics.
bestPractices: Use const / final for read-only variables.
memoryTricks: Think of scopes as Russian nesting dolls — inside dolls can see outside, but outside cannot see inside.
quickRevision: Variables store data. Loops repeat blocks. Functions encapsulate tasks. Objects bundle states.
